content:
In this latest installment of GIJN Toolbox, we highlight three new powerful but easy-to-use investigative tools that have been developed with the needs of journalists in mind.

These were showcased at the recent NICAR26 data journalism summit in the US.

title: From Memoir to Fiction, Investigative Chronicles to Thematic Deep Dives: Books from Perugia
content:
Perhaps it’s the Etruscan architectural remnants, or all the gold-leaf, the art galleries majoring in Renaissance painting, or the mountain vistas, but the International Journalism Festival at Perugia certainly feels very cultural.

This year’s lineup featured live theater performances, documentary screenings, discussions on game design for journalists, and panels about turning your investigation into a book.

“Reading fiction can inform and make you a better nonfiction writer.” — Simon & Schuster editor Dawn Davis

In conversations about where the industry is going next, or how we can reach new audiences, and in fireside chats about the big challenges we all face, many people reached for nonfiction books that have inspired them recently. Elsewhere, when asked what book was on their bedside table, editors and repor
snippet: Perhaps it’s the Etruscan architectural remnants, or all the gold-leaf, the art galleries majoring in Renaissance painting, or the mountain vistas, but the International Journalism Festival at Perugia
creator: Laura Dixon

title: Climate Journalism: Free Online Course on Investigative Reporting for Every Beat
content:
Climate change now runs through every reporting beat, from energy and politics to food systems, health and local news, making it one of the most complex stories journalists can investigate today. Yet, despite its reach, it remains one of the most challenging topics to cover, especially when it comes to following power, money and accountability across systems and borders. To tackle this growing issue, the Knight Center for Journalism in the Americas and the Global Investigative Journalism Network (GIJN) have joined forces to offer a new free online course in English and Spanish.

This climate journalism training course is designed for journalists and editors who want to deepen their understanding of climate issues, whether or not they have covered the topic before. It provides foundational knowledge for those new to climate reporting and offers advanced tools and approaches for more experienced journalists.

Over four weeks, participa

creator: GIJN Staff

title: Research and Investigations Officer
content:
Transparency International (UK) are looking for a Research and Investigations Officer.

You will be responsible for producing new knowledge, insights and high-quality written material, at pace, to support our strategic advocacy objectives.

You will keep abreast of developments in their field, and build expertise to inform solutions for us to advocate to key stakeholders, particularly those in parliaments and governments across the UK.

The Research and Investigations Officer will report to the Head of Research and Investigations and perform this within the UK Programme, focusing on corruption in the UK.

See vacancy

creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Social Media Video Producer
content:
This role is all about making The Free Press’ video content go viral, and growing The Free Press video and podcast audience. You’ll be responsible for taking the videos we produce and making sure it actually travels on social media.

title: Social Media Manager
content:
The Free Press are looking for a social media manager to run the day-to-day execution and long-term vision of their social pages helping to grow The Free Press audience across platforms.

This person should be obsessed with social media, following the latest trends and understanding what makes something go viral across copy, graphics, and video. The ideal candidate will also demonstrate strong proficiency in current events and the modern media ecosystem, understanding how to leverage a fast paced news cycle for virality and impact. Familiarity with/the ability to adapt to The Free Press brand/voice is essential.

See vacancy

creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Director, Editorial and Social Video
content:
The Free Press are looking for a creative, dogged, and news-obsessed producer to join their team. In this role, you’ll work closely with on-screen talent and producers to oversee multiple weekly social videos, news videos, and series, covering current events, domestic politics, foreign policy, culture and tech.

You’ll own the process end-to-end—overseeing weekly social publishing strategy, generating ideas, sharpening pitches, guiding scripts, shaping edits, and delivering videos that are high quality, compelling, and original. This is a role for someone who can move quickly without sacrificing quality, has strong editorial instincts, and knows how to turn complex topics into videos people want to watch.

See vacancy

creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Editor, Climate and Environment
content:
Lighthouse Reports is an award-winning investigative newsroom working with some of the world’s most impactful outlets and creators to deliver deeply reported, public interest stories. We build teams around topics and innovate new approaches to accountability journalism focused on climate, technology, society and conflict.

We’ve worked with more than 200 outlets from around the world ranging from international broadcasters and national newspapers to metro newsrooms and independent creators and filmmakers. We’re a multinational team, working remotely but spending regular, scheduled time together as an organization.

In this role you will work with Lighthouse directors to set a clear strategy for the climate and environment team, maintain a pipeline of viable ideas, meet production targets, manage budgets and reporters, partnerships and projects. You will curate relevant topics and nurture a communal sense of where the landing space is for brilliant investigative ideas.

You will lead a diverse team of outstanding reporters and story editors, channeling Lighthouse’s strengths from data and digital investigations to financial forensics. You will build collaborativ
snippet: Lighthouse Reports is an award-winning investigative newsroom working with some of the world’s most impactful outlets and creators to deliver deeply reported, public interest stories. We build teams a
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: How Just an Email’s Tone Can Threaten Collaboration — and Other Lessons Learned from Seven Years of Multi-Partner Investigations
content:
The best kinds of collaborations can feel effortless: Where every partner is heard and pulls their weight, where differences elevate discussions and the final investigation is greater than the sum of everyone’s work.

But seasoned editors know just how much effort they involve. It takes grit and otherworldly calm to power through the new variables that collaborative investigations constantly throw up. There is no universal template to follow, even when journalists are united by a desire to expose injustice and seed change.

We asked our partners what they wanted from a collaborative investigation, and how our processes helped or failed them.

At Lighthouse Reports — which was founded in the Netherlands in 2019 and which now publishes an average of 12 investigations a year — members of our impact team reach out to partners for feedback after each investigation. These debriefs help us document disputes that are invariably played down after publicati
snippet: The best kinds of collaborations can feel effortless: Where every partner is heard and pulls their weight, where differences elevate discussions and the final investigation is greater than the sum of
creator: Hui Yee Tan

title: The Basics of Follow the Money
content:
“Follow the Money” is a practical, in-depth course designed to equip journalists with the core skills needed to trace financial flows and uncover the economic forces behind corruption, organized crime, and abuse of power. Participants will learn how money moves through companies, bank accounts, contracts, and offshore structures. The key is to see how these trails can reveal who really benefits from wrongdoing. Grounded in proven investigative techniques, the course emphasizes turning complex financial data into clear, evidence-based findings that build stories with real public impact.

This hands-on course introduces you to key tools and methods, terminology, who are stakeholders and where to find them, and where the data is hidden, online and offline.

You will learn how to analyze financial records, company registries, public procurement data, and cross-border transactions. Journalists will strengthe
snippet: “Follow the Money” is a practical, in-depth course designed to equip journalists with the core skills needed to trace financial flows and uncover the economic forces behind corruption, organized crime
creator: GIJN Staff

title: Open Source Databases by Country
content:
This resource contains links to open-source information organized by country.

There are links to open data portals, company registries, government procurement data, sources for information on people and vehicles, and much more.

These databases (click on map below to see resources by country) is provided by the US-based social purpose organization, UNISHKA, which focuses on promoting transparency in government and businesses.

title: Abzas Media: From Exile, Fighting for Press Freedom and Civil Space in Azerbaijan
content:
Mission and Approach

Founded in 2016 by young civil society members in Azerbaijan, Abzas Media was created to report on issues that are often ignored or suppressed in state-controlled media, including corruption, misuse of public resources, and human rights violations.

Abzas Media is especially proud of their imprisoned colleagues who have continued to write and send articles and even do investigations from prison.

Their investigations have included reporting on corruption and financial networks connected to the Aliyev regime. Investigative reports such as How Did a High-Ranking Azerbaijan Official Acquire a Multi-Million Property in France? and
snippet: Mission and Approach Founded in 2016 by young civil society members in Azerbaijan, Abzas Media was created to report on issues that are often ignored or suppressed in state-controlled media, including
creator: Global Forum for Media Development

title: Data Investigator
content:
Global Witness are looking for a Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and support the delivery of a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas wells in Europe.

You will work with the Senior Data Investigator to help develop the core database underpinning the project’s public-facing map, and support data analysis and investigations that uncover the environmental, financial and public interest issues linked to abandoned oil and gas wells.

You will also contribute to the wider work of the Data Investigations Team, supporting the development and delivery of other data-led investigations, strengthening data investigation practice across the organisation, and helping ensure that data is used effectively in Global Witness’s publications and advocacy.

See vacancy
snippet: Global Witness are looking for a Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and support the delivery of a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas wells in Europe.
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Senior Data Investigator
content:
Global Witness are looking for a Senior Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and play a leading role in delivering a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas wells in Europe.

You will lead the development of the core database underpinning the project’s public-facing map, and carry out rigorous data analysis and investigations that help uncover the environmental, financial and public interest issues linked abandoned to oil and gas wells.

You will also contribute to the wider work of the Data Investigations Team, supporting the development and delivery of other data-led investigations, strengthening data investigation practice across the organisation, and helping ensure that data is used effectively in Global Witness’s publications and advocacy.

See vacancy
snippet: Global Witness are looking for a Senior Data Investigator to join their Data Investigations Team and play a leading role in delivering a major new project focused on abandoned and orphaned oil and gas
creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Earth Journalism Network Editorial Assistant
content:
Reporting to the EJN Senior Program Officer, the Editorial Assistant will be based in the UK and work with the EJN global team. The Editorial Assistant will be responsible for supporting the editorial and communications team with copywriting, content production and editorial tasks for the EJN website, newsletter and social media channels. They will also support with programmatic work including reviewing grant applications, onboarding grantees and administrative tasks.

The Editorial Assistant will be comfortable with both working independently and liaising between the various EJN teams and personnel working around the world, which will occasionally require joining calls and meetings during evening hours. This is a full-time position expected to start in May 2026 and last for an estimated 10 months, with the possibility of being extended.

See vacancy

creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Research and Outreach Officer (India)
content:
The Research and Outreach Officer will play a supporting role across the design, coordination, and implementation of research and outreach activities under the Break the Chain project, for strengthening local information systems in India. Working closely with senior team members, the role will help translate project plans into effective field execution by supporting research activities, coordinating stakeholder engagement, and ensuring smooth delivery of on-ground interventions. The Officer will assist in operationalising research designs, coordinating fieldwork, supporting data collection and presentation, coordinating with teams dealing with social listening, while also facilitating outreach with community members, local media, and partner organisations.

The role involves strong coordination, ensuring timely implementation, addressing field-level challenges, and maintaining quality and ethical standards. Overall, this position is suited for someone who is organised, proactive, and comfortable working at the intersection of research and field engagement, with the ability to support end-to-end execution and contribute to effective programme delivery. creator: Emily O'Sullivan

title: Writer
content:
Our World in Data is looking for an experienced writer. The ideal candidate already writes single-authored pieces regularly and publicly for a non-specialist audience, builds arguments based on data and research, and has the range to cover many of the topics they work on. This is a senior role: the person hired will work closely with Hannah Ritchie, their Deputy Editor. The hiring process involves a paid writing residency before any full-time offer.
